article_title: US New EV Sales Fall 28 Percent in Q1 2026 After Tax Credit Expiration as Used Market Surges to Near Price Parity

findings.message: This article was requested by a human editor — apply heightened scrutiny to content accuracy and source quality

content_preview.title: US New EV Sales Fall 28 Percent in Q1 2026 After Tax Credit Expiration as Used Market Surges to Near Price Parity
content_preview.summary: Cox Automotive data shows new EV sales dropped to 212,600 units in Q1 2026, while used EV sales climbed 12 percent to 93,500 units with prices approaching parity with gas vehicles.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

New electric vehicle sales in the United States fell 28 percent year-over-year in the first quarter of 2026, dropping to approximately 212,600 units from 296,304 in Q1 2025, according to [Cox Automotive data reported by Electrek](https://electrek.co/2026/03/27/used-ev-sales-boom-new-ev-sales-drop-28-percent-q1-2026/). The decline follows the expiration of the $7,500 federal EV tax credit on September 30, 2025, and marks the sharpest quarterly contraction in the modern EV era. At the...

editor_notes.content_quality: Data-rich EV market analysis with excellent new vs used market comparison. Good use of specific numbers from Cox Automotive data.
editor_notes.source_verification: Electrek #1 (fetched): Confirms 212,600 units, -28% YoY, 296,304 Q1 2025, 5.8% share, 7.5% Q3 2025 peak, 130 days inventory, $55,300 avg price, $6,500 gap, Tesla 122,196/-4.6%, used 93,500/+12%/+17%, $34,821 avg used EV, $33,487 gas, $1,300 gap, 42 vs 38 days, hybrids +57% 756K Q4 2025, lease returns, $7,500 credit Sept 30 2025 expiry. Electrek #2 (fetched): Confirms Tesla Q1 consensus 365,645, +8% YoY, -12.5% sequential. Note: article says 57.5% BEV share for Tesla but source shows 3.3% overall market share — BEV-specific share figure plausible but not explicitly confirmed in extraction.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All major data points verified. Honda internal link to 2026-03-19 article confirmed. Minor: Tesla BEV share 57.5% figure not explicitly found in extraction but is consistent with 122,196 out of ~212,600 total BEV sales.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and analytical. Good contextualization of the 28% decline against market stabilization narrative.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Q1 2026 EV sales coverage. Complements Honda EV cancellation article.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong human-requested EV market submission with comprehensive data verification. Ready for publication.
